content:
- All required lanes passed: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
- Updated the task markdown and execution plan to completed/checked state with `<passes>true</passes>`, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Next I am staging the task-related code plus Ralph state, committing with test evidence in the message, and pushing to the current branch.

- Finished the task end to end: code landed, task/plan updated, `.ralph/task_switch.sh` ran, commit `3240d2e` was created, and pushed to `master`.
- The delete long-lane now proves helper-shadow absence precedes real-table deletion, remains stable across later reconcile passes, and verifies only the real migrated table.
- All required lanes passed before completion: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`. Remaining worktree dirt is unrelated and intentionally left unstaged.

- Read prior progress, refreshed the Ralph task summaries, and checked active tasks against the choose-task rules.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, no visible blocker tags, and no priority overrides among the remaining failing tasks.
- Inspected the actual repo shape: Rust 2024 workspace with `crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`, and `crates/ingest-contract`, driven by Cargo through `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, and `make test-long`.
- Compared the earliest unfinished stories directly. Chose story-10 baseline E2E task 04 because it closes a core realism gap in migration coverage (composite PKs plus excluded tables) before larger multi-db, chaos, integrity, documentation, report, or CI work.
- Next immediate step after this context ends: work `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/04-task-e2e-composite-pk-and-excluded-table-handling.md` with the `tdd` skill, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the relevant Ralph instructions for this turn.
- Loaded the `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills so planning/execution follows the required workflow and boundary cleanup expectations.
- Next step is reading the current task markdown plus any existing plan files to decide whether this turn must plan-and-quit or execute-and-verify.

- Determined the current task has no `<plan>` entry yet, so this turn must create the plan file and task linkage rather than execute code.
- Read prior story-10 plan/task conventions and the existing long-lane harness/tests to anchor the new composite-PK plus excluded-table plan in real repo boundaries.
- Next I am reading the generic e2e harness and reconcile/verify-related tests to identify the most likely boundary smell and write a concrete `NOW EXECUTE` plan.

- Read the current task, confirmed it had no existing `<plan>` entry, and treated this turn as a required planning-only handoff.
- Wrote `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/04-task-e2e-composite-pk-and-excluded-table-handling_plans/2026-04-19-composite-pk-excluded-table-e2e-plan.md` using the `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` constraints, with an explicit mixed-schema E2E plan for included composite-PK tables plus intentionally ignored excluded tables.
- Updated the task markdown to include the new `<plan>` path. The plan now ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so the next turn should execute the slices, tick plan/task boxes as work completes, and only finish after `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Read startup progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-10-e2e-baseline/04-task-e2e-composite-pk-and-excluded-table-handling.md`, and prior progress says its linked plan already ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn should execute rather than re-plan.
- Next I am reading the task markdown and linked plan in full, then I will implement the slices with red-green TDD and finish only after `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Read the active task and linked execution plan in full. The plan ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn is implementing the scenario and driving the required validation lanes.
- Inspected the current long-lane boundary. `crates/runner/tests/default_bootstrap_long_lane.rs` already mixes multiple schemas and inline SQL, while `default_bootstrap_harness.rs` is customers-specific. The clear cleanup is to add a dedicated support module for the composite-PK plus exclusion scenario instead of bloating the test file.
- Next I am mining existing contract tests for composite-PK helper-table and verify behavior, then I will add the first failing long-lane slice and the new support boundary.

- Added the new ignored long-lane entry point and intentionally ran the first red. It failed because the dedicated support boundary file did not exist yet, which was the expected TDD failure.
- Added `crates/runner/tests/support/composite_pk_exclusion_harness.rs` plus one generic helper-table inventory waiter in `e2e_harness.rs`, keeping the composite/exclusion schema, helper-shadow assertions, and excluded-table checks out of `default_bootstrap_long_lane.rs`.
- The targeted ignored lane `cargo test -p runner ignored_long_lane_handles_composite_primary_keys_while_skipping_unselected_tables -- --ignored` now passes on the real Docker/bootstrap/runner path. Next I am running the required repository validation lanes: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
